summary: Scope cross-workspace sync fan-out to the active workspace on BOTH surfaces — the generated dependent-resource sync AND the novel module-membership enrichment — so neither enumerates other workspaces' parents and dials them under the active slug (403 per foreign parent).
reason: The local store is multi-tenant (one data.db can hold projects from several enrolled workspaces). (1) dependentParentRows() read EVERY project via store.ListIDs("projects") with no workspace filter, so the dependent phase (states/cycles/modules/labels/issues) dialed /workspaces/<active-slug>/projects/<foreign-project>/... and drew a 403 for each foreign project. (2) The post-sync module enrichment (localModules) walked EVERY module across workspaces and, worse, aborted the whole enrichment on the first foreign-module 403 — so the active workspace's module membership was never built. Live A/B on a copy of the prod DB: old binary = 5 cross-workspace 403 (the 5 doctor-school projects) + enrichment aborted; new binary = 0 dependent 403, 0 enrichment 403, and enrichment now completes (8 modules / 77 links / 77 issues patched).
change: store.go: add Store.ListIDsScoped(resourceType, scopeColumn, scopeValue) — ListIDs plus an optional tenant filter (typed-column path validated via validIdentifierRE + pragma_table_info, bound scopeValue; json_extract fallback on the generic resources table). Empty scope == ListIDs.

sync.go: add parentTenantScopeColumns{"projects":"workspace"} and resolveActiveWorkspaceID(slug, []config.WorkspaceEntry) which maps the active slug to its UUID via the local [[workspaces]] registry. RunE resolves the active workspace UUID from c.Config and threads it through syncDependentResources -> syncDependentResource -> dependentParentRows, which now scopes the single-id parent enumeration via ListIDsScoped. Unknown UUID (slug not enrolled / no cached id) => scopeValue"" => unscoped fallback (old behavior, 403-as-warning safety net intact).

modules_novel.go: localModules + enrichModuleMembership take a workspaceID; when no single --project filter is given they scope modules and the module_ids reset to `projects_id IN (SELECT id FROM projects WHERE workspace = ?)` (modules carry no workspace column, so scope via the parent project). Both call sites resolve workspaceID via resolveActiveWorkspaceID. Per-module access denials are now non-fatal (skip & continue) so a stray foreign-module 403 in the unscoped fallback can no longer abort enrichment for every other module.

review hardening (Greptile #1350 4/5): (a) ListIDsScoped now DEGRADES to unscoped ListIDs when the typed table exists but lacks the scope column (was: json_extract probe on generic resources that could silently return zero parents); json_extract is kept only for the no-typed-table case. (b) enrichModuleMembership moved the stale-link DELETE to run only after the first SUCCESSFUL module-issues page, so a 403 before any page no longer wipes a module's cached junction rows in the unscoped fallback (the prior break+commit committed the delete without repopulation).
validated_outcome: go build ./... + go vet + go test ./internal/cli ./internal/store all green. Live A/B against a copy of the prod data.db (isolated USERPROFILE home, prod untouched): old installed binary emitted 5 cross-workspace 403 on the doctor-school project IDs and aborted module enrichment; rebuilt binary emitted 0 dependent 403, 0 enrichment 403, and completed enrichment (8 modules / 77 links / 77 issues). bbm data synced identically (states total 20 both runs).

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Generator-level tenant-scoped dependent fan-out: drive the parent-table tenant-scope column from a spec annotation (e.g. x-pp-tenant-scope-column) plus a tenant-value resolver, so any multi-tenant printed CLI sharing one local store scopes dependent enumeration without a hand patch.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: Today the generated dependent-sync reads ALL parent rows; multi-tenant scoping is API-specific (slug->UUID via a local registry here) and not yet modeled by the profiler/templates.
